steps.title: State the trade-off
steps.content: The researchers concluded that increased competition drove toxin production at the expense of growth. Supporting data must therefore show higher competition linked to more bufadienolides and lower body mass.

steps.title: Reject partial evidence
steps.content: Choice A discusses body mass but not toxin production. Choice C focuses on toxin measures without showing the growth trade-off. Choice D discusses toxin variety rather than production.

steps.title: Choose both variables
steps.content: The data comparing low and medium density with high density show lower toxin concentrations but greater body masses under less competition, directly supporting the trade-off. The answer is B.
